content.problemStatement:
Alice jogs everyday to keep herself fit and active. She noticed that she burns XXX calories when jogging the first kilometer, and for K>1K > 1K>1, jogging the KthK^{th}Kth kilometer burns calories equivalent to the total number of calories burned while jogging the first K−1K-1K−1 kilometers.

What is the total number of calories that Alice burns after jogging for NNN kilometers? The answer can be very large, so report it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7 (100000000710000000071000000007).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the total number of calories that Alice burns after jogging for NNN kilometers, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and X.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing (X·2^(N‑1)) modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case the answer is X·2^(N‑1) modulo 1e9+7.
Case 1: 2·2^0 = 2.
Case 2: 1·2^1 = 2.
Case 3: 1·2^12547 mod 1e9+7 = 588809226.

solution.approach: The calories burned in the first kilometre are X. For K>1 the K‑th kilometre burns the total calories burnt in the first K‑1 kilometres. This yields the recurrence a1 = X, aK = sum_{i=1}^{K-1} a_i. From the recurrence we obtain aK = 2^{K-2}·X for K≥2. The total after N kilometres is S_N = X·2^{N-1}. Hence each answer is X multiplied by 2 to the power (N‑1) modulo M = 1 000 000 007. Compute the power with binary exponentiation in O(log N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log N) – each test case uses binary exponentiation.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few 64‑bit variables are stored.

code.Python3:
import sys
MOD = 1_000_000_007

def mod_pow(base, exp):
    res = 1
    base %= MOD
    while exp:
        if exp & 1:
            res = (res * base) % MOD
        base = (base * base) % MOD
        exp >>= 1
    return res

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        X = int(next(it))
        ans = (X % MOD) * mod_pow(2, N - 1) % MOD
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
